task.query: Counts all business days in a current month.

record.query: Counts all business days in a current month.

command: first=$(date +%Y-%m-01); last=$(date -d "$(date +%Y-%m-01) +1 month -1 day" +%Y-%m-%d); seq $(date -d "$first" +%s) $(date -d "$last" +%s) 86400 | xargs -I{} date -d @{} +%u | awk '{if($1<6) c++} END{print c}'

generation.generated_command: first=$(date +%Y-%m-01); last=$(date -d "$(date +%Y-%m-01) +1 month -1 day" +%Y-%m-%d); seq $(date -d "$first" +%s) $(date -d "$last" +%s) 86400 | xargs -I{} date -d @{} +%u | awk '{if($1<6) c++} END{print c}'

raw_output_payload.content: first=$(date +%Y-%m-01); last=$(date -d "$(date +%Y-%m-01) +1 month -1 day" +%Y-%m-%d); seq $(date -d "$first" +%s) $(date -d "$last" +%s) 86400 | xargs -I{} date -d @{} +%u | awk '{if($1<6) c++} END{print c}'

We need a bash command that counts business days (Mon-Fri) in current month. Use date, seq, etc. One-liner. Use GNU date. Compute first day and last day. Then loop. Count days where weekday not 6 or 7. Use awk. Let's craft: 

```
first=$(date +%Y-%m-01); last=$(date -d "$(date +%Y-%m-01) +1 month -1 day" +%Y-%m-%d); seq -f "%F" -s " " $(date -d "$first" +%s) $(date -d "$last" +%s) 86400 | awk 'BEGIN{c=0} {if($0!~/(Sat|Sun)/){c++}} END{print c}'
```

But need to check weekday. Use date -d "$d" +%u gives 1-7. So:

```

```

But seq with dates? seq expects numbers. Use seq $(date -d "$first" +%s) $(date -d "$last" +%s) 86400. Then format with date -d @epoch +%F. So:

```

```
